quality_gate.note: playwright_unavailable — Chrome could not launch (existing browser session conflict on Windows). All three suites evaluated via static analysis.

checks.evidence: templates table has all required columns: id UUID PK, workspace_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, category TEXT, template_data JSONB NOT NULL, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. update_updated_at() trigger reused from migration 015 as documented. Index on workspace_id present.
All 4 policies (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) use can_access_workspace(workspace_id). INSERT uses WITH CHECK, others use USING.

checks.check: Template TypeScript type matches migration columns
checks.evidence: types/database.ts Template interface: id/workspace_id/name/description/category/template_data/created_by/created_at/updated_at — 1:1 match with migration 020 DDL. Sub-types TemplateData/TemplateSectionData/TemplateStepData/TemplateConnectionData/TemplateStepRoleData all defined.

checks.check: API GET /api/v1/templates — envelope pattern and auth guard
checks.evidence: templates/route.ts GET: auth guard present, workspace_id required, successResponse(templates) envelope, errorResponse for all failure paths.

checks.check: API POST /api/v1/templates — creates from section, builds template_data
checks.evidence: POST validates workspace_id/section_id/name. Fetches section+steps+connections+step_roles. Builds TemplateData with relative step positions (position_x - section.position_x). Inserts with created_by=user.id. Returns 201 with template row.

checks.check: API DELETE /api/v1/templates/[id] — envelope and RLS enforcement
checks.evidence: templates/[id]/route.ts DELETE: auth guard, .delete().select().single() lets RLS block unauthorized deletes, PGRST116 maps to 404, successResponse({ deleted: true }) returned.

checks.check: Deploy route returns { section_id } and uses UUID remapping
checks.evidence: templates/[id]/deploy/route.ts: builds stepIdMap (Map<string,string>) mapping old template step IDs to new DB step IDs. Connections remapped via stepIdMap.get(). Returns successResponse({ section_id: newSection.id }, 201).

client.ts: fetchTemplates → GET /api/v1/templates?workspace_id=; createTemplate → POST /api/v1/templates; deployTemplate → POST /api/v1/templates/${id}/deploy returning { section_id }; deleteTemplate → DELETE /api/v1/templates/${id}. All use apiFetch<T>() correctly.

lib/templates.ts: 4 templates (Customer Onboarding, Support Ticket, Content Creation, Lead Nurturing). Each has name/description/category/template_data. Steps via makeSteps() have no id field (id is optional in TemplateStepData). connections/step_roles are empty arrays — deploy route handles missing ids with if (templateStep.id) guard.

Deploy route fetches all teams in target workspace → all roles in those teams → builds roleNameMap (name→id). step_roles matched by role_name, silently skipped if unmatched. stepIdMap used to translate step IDs. stepRoleInserts filtered of nulls before insert.

flow-canvas.tsx: 504 lines (4 lines over threshold). All other components under threshold: step-detail-panel.tsx 461, canvas-view.tsx 353, section-detail-panel.tsx 341, coloring-panel.tsx 347, step-node.tsx 143, section-node.tsx 94. client.ts 750 lines but is an API client not a component.

checks.check: useCallback/useMemo on handlers passed as props
checks.evidence: flow-canvas.tsx: handleNodesChange, handleEdgesChange, handleConnect, handleNodeClick, handlePaneClick, handleAddStep, handleAddSection, handleKeyDown, handleExportPdf, handleClick all wrapped in useCallback. initialNodes/initialEdges wrapped in useMemo.

checks.check: Inline object/array literals in JSX props (re-render risk)
checks.evidence: nodeTypes defined as module-level constant (not inline) — avoids React Flow re-render issue. No inline nodeTypes={...} found in flow-canvas.tsx JSX.

checks.check: Canvas node rendering — React.memo on custom nodes
checks.evidence: StepNode, SectionNode have no React.memo wrapping. React Flow recommends memo on custom nodes to prevent unnecessary re-renders when parent state changes. Partially mitigated by module-level nodeTypes and React Flow's internal memoization, but explicit memo would be safer.

checks.check: Client-side data fetching patterns — N+1 risks
checks.evidence: deploy/route.ts (server-side): sequential INSERT loop for templateData.steps — each step gets its own supabase.from('steps').insert().select('id').single() call. For a template with N steps = N sequential DB roundtrips. Acceptable for current template sizes (5 steps), but would degrade for large templates. A bulk insert + ID matching approach would be more efficient.

checks.check: Import graph — circular deps or heavy deps loaded eagerly
checks.evidence: flow-canvas.tsx imported in canvas-view.tsx, canvas-view.tsx imported in page.tsx and public share page. No circular imports detected. No client-side heavy deps (e.g., PDF libs) eagerly imported outside dynamic imports.

regression-baseline-full.note: Chrome browser could not launch — process exited immediately with 'Opening in existing browser session' (Windows Chrome session conflict). Browser install attempted, issue persisted. Regression baseline testing skipped. Recommend re-running tester with no Chrome instances open, or switching to Playwright-managed Chromium browser.

improvements_found.observation: Deploy route inserts steps in a sequential for-of loop — N supabase INSERT calls for N steps. For templates with many steps this creates N database roundtrips on the server.
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Batch insert all steps in one call, then build the ID map from the returned rows using the stored template step IDs as a correlation key. Requires templateData steps to have their original IDs (already optional in the schema — populated when saving from a section).

improvements_found.observation: Custom React Flow node components (StepNode, SectionNode, StageNode, TouchpointNode) are not wrapped in React.memo. React Flow's nodeTypes pattern requires stable references, but the components themselves will still re-render on any parent context change.
improvements_found.design_principle: Performance: memoize components with stable props
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Wrap each custom node component with React.memo: export const StepNode = React.memo(function StepNode(...) { ... }). React Flow's NodeRenderer will skip re-renders when node data hasn't changed.

improvements_found.observation: flow-canvas.tsx is 504 lines, 4 lines over the 500-line threshold. Minor overage but worth tracking.
improvements_found.design_principle: Maintainability: keep files focused and under 500 lines
improvements_found.suggested_approach: The coloring-related state and handlers (lines ~380-500) could be extracted into a useColoringPanel hook or moved to the coloring-panel component itself.

notes: This was a testing-only iteration (no builder tasks). Playwright unavailable for browser regression. Two of three suites completed via static analysis: feat-052-data-integrity (10/10 checks pass), performance-static-audit (3/6 pass, 3 warnings). regression-baseline-full deferred — requires browser access. Static findings confirm FEAT-052 data layer is correctly implemented and safe to proceed to [2/2] UI work.
